short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · next earnings cycle)
bullets: Run-rate $8.00B in FY2026 Q1, +17.5% YoY — accelerating trajectory entering the next print.
Next-quarter consensus: $15.30B (+91.3% vs current Q) — set by 24 broker contributors.
EPS consensus next qtr: $7.25.
Margin context: 8.2% net margin — stable.
Next earnings: 2026-08-13 — primary catalyst for the short-term window.
short_term.takeaway: Going into the next earnings print, the setup is constructive — consensus is achievable based on current run-rate.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · multi-year setup)
bullets: TTM revenue $36B — in cyclical drawdown.
Sell-side: FY1 $56B → FY2 $60B (+7.2% multi-year top-line implied).
Forward EBIT margin: 15.0% — average operating profile over the long-cycle view.
6-quarter run-rate CAGR: -34.6% — the secular trajectory the market is pricing.
Sector thesis (Industrials): Reshoring + defense supercycle drives a multi-year backlog conversion story.
long_term.takeaway: Long-cycle thesis is intact if the next 2–4 quarters confirm a stabilization in trajectory.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–30 days · trading window)
bullets: Price $529.15 vs SMA50 $576.29 (-8.2%) — short-term trend is bearish (price has lost the SMA50 line).
RSI(14): 29.4 — oversold — possible bounce setup.
MACD: -9.93 vs signal -4.84 — bearish crossover.
30-day return: -6.0% — directional bias entering the next 4 weeks.
Bollinger band position: -9% of band width (band width 12.1% — normal).
Near-term S/R: resistance ~$545.02, support ~$513.28. 40% of 52w range (low $439.11 → high $662.49).
short_term.takeaway: Short-term trader bias: short (trend + momentum aligned bearish).

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ days · positioning window)
bullets: Price $529.15 vs SMA200 $520.67 (+1.6%) — long-term trend is in **neutral**.
Moving average regime: Golden cross active (SMA50 > SMA200) — institutional trend filter bullish.
1-year return: +2.8% — range-bound.

90-day return: -14.6% — intermediate-term weakness.
long_term.takeaway: Long-term positioning: accumulate on weakness — structural trend supportive.

short_term.title: Short-term view (0–45 DTE · front-month tactical)
bullets: Front-month ATM IV: 58.2% — elevated (premium-selling favored).
Put/Call OI: 1.12 — balanced flow.
30-day implied move: ±6.5% ($494.91 – $563.39) — the 1-σ range the options market is pricing.
ATM skew: -26.16pp — call skew (upside bid — bullish positioning).
Front-month OI cluster: $525.0 — likely magnet / pin-risk for expiry.
short_term.strategy: Calendar spread at $529 strike: short 30 DTE / long 60 DTE. Mid IV, range-bound → harvest near-term theta while keeping back-month optionality.

long_term.title: Long-term view (90+ DTE · LEAPS / structural)
bullets: Back-month avg IV (90+ DTE): 32.6% across 5 expiries.
Available long-dated expiries: 5 dates spanning 119–609 DTE.
210-day implied move: ±24.7% ($398.36 – $659.94) — the long-dated 1-σ range.
Sell-side PT vs options-implied range: consensus $520 (-1.7% upside). Compare to the 90+ DTE implied range above.
Term structure in backwardation (-25.7pp front→back) — near-term event premium; long-dated vol cheaper.
long_term.strategy: Sell 6-month strangle: short $476 put / short $582 call. Range-bound + high IV → premium-selling on both sides; profits if spot stays within ±10%; manage with rolls or assignment.
